context.description: The Google Cloud Run deployment flow reached production, but two deployment-specific configuration issues remained: Artifact Registry tag immutability conflicted with `latest` pushes, and backend CORS still allowed only the local development origin.

problem.summary: Cloud Run production deployment was unstable because build-time and runtime configuration drifted from production requirements.

details.issue: The Cloud Run Kaniko jobs pushed both commit-specific tags and `latest`, but the target Artifact Registry repository had tag immutability enabled.
The backend service continued to emit `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://localhost:3000`, which blocked browser requests from the actual deployed frontend origin.

problem.expected_behavior: Production image publishing should succeed against immutable Artifact Registry repositories, and deployed frontend/browser traffic should be accepted by backend CORS without requiring manual follow-up edits after each deploy.
problem.actual_behavior: Pipeline jobs failed during image push with `MANIFEST_INVALID`, and successful frontend deployments still could not call backend APIs because preflight requests were rejected.

task.title: Align Artifact Registry tagging with immutability

requirements.requirement: Do not push `latest` for Cloud Run Artifact Registry images
Use immutable commit-based image tags for backend and frontend deploy jobs

task.title: Keep backend CORS aligned with deployed frontend origin

requirements.requirement: Allow backend CORS configuration to contain multiple origins
Update backend Cloud Run service CORS origin list automatically after frontend deployment
Preserve local development origin access unless explicitly replaced

acceptance_criteria.criteria: Cloud Run image publishing no longer fails on immutable Artifact Registry repositories
Frontend Cloud Run can call backend Cloud Run APIs without CORS preflight rejection
Production deployment does not require manual CORS origin patching after each frontend rollout
